content.text:
<attached_files>

<code_selection path="file:///c%3A/Users/12263/.cursor/plans/kb_upload_i18n_82b3a545.plan.md" lines="1-197">
     1|---
     2|name: KB Upload i18n
     3|overview: 增强知识库管理页面：支持拖拽上传 PDF 直接入库、多知识库选择与文档管理，并为整个 UI 添加英文 / 繁体中文 / 简体中文三语言切换。
     4|todos:
     5|  - id: fix-rag-ingest-source
     6|    content: 修改 rag_ingest.py：将 _make_doc 和 load_pdf_with_pypdf 中的 source 字段改为包含文件名（如 pdf_mineru:filename.pdf），并将核心函数 load_pdf_documents/embed_and_insert/ensure_collection 改为可被 ui.py 直接 import
     7|    status: pending
     8|  - id: add-i18n
     9|    content: 在 ui.py 头部添加 TRANSLATIONS 字典（EN / 繁中 / 简中），覆盖所有 UI 文字标签
    10|    status: pending
    11|  - id: add-kb-backend
    12|    content: 在 ui.py 添加新后端函数：list_kb_collections、create_kb_collection、list_kb_documents、delete_kb_documents、upload_and_ingest
    13|    status: pending
    14|  - id: redesign-kb-tab
    15|    content: 重新设计知识库管理 Tab：拖拽上传区（gr.File）、KB 选择下拉、新建 KB、文档列表（gr.Dataframe）、删除按钮、入库日志
    16|    status: pending
    17|  - id: add-lang-switcher
    18|    content: 在页面顶部添加语言切换 Radio，绑定 on_lang_change 事件刷新所有组件 label/value
    19|    status: pending
    20|  - id: restart-ui
    21|    content: 重启 nat-ui 容器使改动生效，并在浏览器中验证拖拽上传和语言切换功能
    22|    status: pending
    23|isProject: false
    24|---
    25|
    26|# 知识库管理升级 + 多语言支持
    27|
    28|## 改动范围（仅 2 个文件）
    29|
    30|- `[ui.py](c:\Dev\capstoneProject\ui.py)` — 主要改动
    31|- `[rag_ingest.py](c:\Dev\capstoneProject\rag_ingest.py)` — 小改动：修复 `source` 字段包含文件名
    32|
    33|---
    34|
    35|## 一、`rag_ingest.py` 改动（小）
    36|
    37|`_make_doc` 和 `load_pdf_with_pypdf` 中的 `source` 字段改为包含文件名，便于后续按文件删除：
    38|
    39|```python
    40|# 修复前
    41|"source": "pdf_mineru"
    42|# 修复后
    43|"source": f"pdf_mineru:{pdf_name}"   # 例：pdf_mineru:manupremier-protector.pdf
    44|```
    45|
    46|同时将 `load_pdf_documents` 函数导出为可调用函数（已是，无需改动），供 `ui.py` 直接调用（而非 subprocess）。
    47|
    48|---
    49|
    50|## 二、`ui.py` 改动（主要）
    51|
    52|### 2A. i18n 翻译字典
    53|
    54|在文件头部加入 `TRANSLATIONS` 字典，覆盖所有 UI 文字：
    55|
    56|```python
    57|TRANSLATIONS = {
    58|    "en": {
    59|        "app_title": "Insurance RAG Q&A System",
    60|        "tab_chat": "Chat",
    61|        "tab_kb": "Knowledge Base",
    62|        "tab_xhs": "Xiaohongshu Data",
    63|        "tab_help": "Help",
    64|        "send_btn": "Send",
    65|        "clear_btn": "Clear Chat",
    66|        "kb_upload_label": "Drag & Drop PDF files here",
    67|        "kb_select_label": "Select Knowledge Base",
    68|        "kb_new_label": "Create New KB",
    69|        "kb_doc_list_label": "Documents in KB",
    70|        "kb_delete_btn": "Delete Selected",
    71|        "kb_ingest_btn": "Start Ingestion",
    72|        "kb_refresh_btn": "Refresh Status",
    73|        # ... 全部字段
    74|    },
    75|    "zh-TW": { ... },   # 繁體中文
    76|    "zh-CN": { ... },   # 简体中文（当前默认）
    77|}
    78|```
    79|
    80|页面顶部添加语言选择器（`gr.Radio`），切换时通过 `gr.update()` 刷新所有组件的 `label` / `value`。
    81|
    82|### 2B. 知识库管理 Tab 完全重设计
    83|
    84|新布局（取代当前简单的两栏）：
    85|
    86|```
    87|┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    88|│  语言切换  [EN] [繁中] [简中]                     │
    89|├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    90|│ Tab: 保险问答 | 知识库管理 | 小红书数据 | 使用说明 │
    91|└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    92|
    93|知识库管理 Tab:
    94|┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┐
    95|│   知识库选择区        │   上传区                   │
    96|│  ┌────────────────┐  │  ┌──────────────────────┐ │
    97|│  │ Dropdown: 选KB │  │  │  拖放 PDF 到这里       │ │
    98|│  └────────────────┘  │  │  gr.File(多文件/拖拽) │ │
    99|│  [+ 新建知识库]       │  └──────────────────────┘ │
   100|│  ☐ 清空旧数据        │  ☐ 使用 MinerU 解析        │
   101|│  [刷新状态]          │  [开始入库]               │
   102|└──────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┘
   103|┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   104|│  当前知识库中的文档（Dataframe）                  │
   105|│  文件名  |  块数  |  入库时间  |  ☐ 选择删除      │
   106|├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   107|│  [删除所选文档]              [入库进度日志]        │
   108|└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   109|```
   110|
   111|### 2C. 新增后端函数
   112|
   113|
   114|| 函数                                                        | 说明                                                                                                 |
   115|| --------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
   116|| `list_kb_collections()`                                   | 查询 Milvus 所有 collections，返回列表                                                                      |
   117|| `create_kb_collection(name)`                              | 在 Milvus 创建指定名称的 collection                                                                        |
   118|| `list_kb_documents(collection)`                           | 查询该 collection 中的唯一 source 值及块数                                                                    |
   119|| `delete_kb_documents(collection, sources)`                | 按 source 字段删除指定文档的所有向量                                                                             |
   120|| `upload_and_ingest(files, collection, use_mineru, clear)` | 保存上传文件到 `/app/data/uploads/`，调用 `rag_ingest.py` 的 `load_pdf_documents` + `embed_and_insert`，流式返回日志 |
   121|
   122|
   123|### 2D. 数据流
   124|
   125|```mermaid
   126|flowchart TD
   127|    User["用户拖拽 PDF"] --> GrFile["gr.File 组件"]
   128|    GrFile --> SaveDisk["保存到 /app/data/uploads/"]
   129|    SaveDisk --> IngestFn["upload_and_ingest()"]
   130|    IngestFn --> LoadPDF["load_pdf_documents()"]
   131|    LoadPDF --> Embed["embed_and_insert() → NVIDIA NIM"]
   132|    Embed --> Milvus["Milvus Collection"]
   133|    Milvus --> DocList["list_kb_documents() → Dataframe 显示"]
   134|    DeleteBtn["删除按钮"] --> DeleteFn["delete_kb_documents()"]
   135|    DeleteFn --> Milvus
   136|    LangSwitch["语言切换 Radio"] --> UpdateLabels["gr.update() 刷新所有标签"]
   137|```
   138|
   139|
   140|
   141|---
   142|
   143|## 三、关键实现细节
   144|
   145|### 拖拽上传（Gradio 5.x）
   146|
   147|```python
   148|pdf_upload = gr.File(
   149|    label="...",
   150|    file_types=[".pdf"],
   151|    file_count="multiple",
   152|    height=120,
   153|)
   154|```
   155|
   156|Gradio 5.x 的 `gr.File` 原生支持拖放，`file_count="multiple"` 允许多文件。
   157|
   158|### 语言切换
   159|
   160|```python
   161|lang_radio = gr.Radio(
   162|    choices=["EN", "繁中", "简中"],
   163|    value="简中",
   164|    label="Language",
   165|    container=False,
   166|)
   167|
   168|def on_lang_change(lang):
   169|    t = TRANSLATIONS[lang]
   170|    return (
   171|        gr.update(label=t["kb_upload_label"]),    # pdf_upload
   172|        gr.update(value=t["kb_ingest_btn"]),       # ingest_btn
   173|        gr.update(label=t["kb_select_label"]),     # kb_selector
   174|        # ... 其余组件
   175|    )
   176|
   177|lang_radio.change(fn=on_lang_change, inputs=lang_radio, outputs=[pdf_upload, ingest_btn, ...])
   178|```
   179|
   180|### 文档删除（Milvus filter expression）
   181|
   182|```python
   183|client.delete(
   184|    collection_name=collection,
   185|    filter=f'source == "pdf_pypdf:{filename}"',
   186|)
   187|```
   188|
   189|---
   190|
   191|## 四、无需修改的文件
   192|
   193|- `Dockerfile` — 不需要新依赖（`google-cloud-storage` 不用了）
   194|- `docker-compose.yml` — `/app/data/uploads` 已通过 `app_data` volume 覆盖
   195|- `xhs_db_mcp.py`, `rag_mcp.py` — 无改动
   196|
   197|
</code_selection>

</attached_files>
<user_query>
知识库管理升级 + 多语言支持

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
</user_query>

Let me start implementing the plan. I'll work through all the todos:

1. Fix rag_ingest.py: source field to include filename
2. Add i18n translations to ui.py
3. Add new backend functions to ui.py
4. Redesign KB management tab
5. Add language switcher
6. Restart container and verify

Let me start with todo 1 (fix-rag-ingest-source) and write the new ui.py simultaneously since they're somewhat independent.
先修改 `rag_ingest.py`，让 `source` 字段包含文件名：
现在完整重写 `ui.py`，一次性完成 todos 2-5（i18n + 后端函数 + KB Tab 重设计 + 语言切换）：
先在容器内测试语法，再重启：
无报错。重启容器：
启动成功，无错误。在浏览器中验证：

页面已完整加载，语言切换按钮（EN/繁中/简中）都存在。截图查看视觉效果：

The page has loaded. I can see:
1. The language radio buttons (EN, 繁中, 简中) with 简中 selected
2. The tabs in Chinese (保险问答, 知识库管理, 小红书数据, 使用说明)
3. The chat tab is active with all elements visible
4. KB management elements are visible in the accessibility tree

Now let me take a screenshot to see the visual layout, and then test:
1. Switch language to EN
2. Check KB management tab
3. Test the upload area
页面结构完全正确。测试语言切换 → EN：

EN 已选中。等服务端处理，再刷新快照：

I see EN is now selected (checked), but the UI labels haven't updated yet - they're still showing Chinese text. This is because the `lang_radio.change` event hasn't fired yet (maybe because the radio button uses `states: [readonly]` which might not trigger the change event properly). 

Wait, actually looking more carefully - the button e0 is now checked (EN is selected), but the labels are still in Chinese. This suggests the `on_lang_change` function was called but the updates haven't been reflected yet, or the function is running but there's an issue.

Actually, the snapshot might be showing the state BEFORE the server processes the change event. 

Let me wait and take another snapshot to see if the language change actually went through.

标签显示还是中文，看日志是否有报错：

The language still shows Chinese text even though EN is selected. The issue is that the `lang_radio.change` event is not updating the UI properly. Let me check the container logs to see if there's an error.

Actually, the accessibility snapshot might not be showing the updated content correctly because Gradio updates the DOM via JavaScript, and the accessibility tree might not reflect the JS-updated content properly.

Let me check the container logs to see if the `on_lang_change` function is being called.

检查 `on_lang_change` 函数本身是否有问题：

No logs. The `on_lang_change` function is not being called. This means the `lang_radio.change` event is not firing. 

Looking at the accessibility snapshot, the radio buttons have `states: [readonly]` which means they're not interactive. This is likely because I'm using `gr.Radio` inside a `gr.Row` and it's being rendered as a static component.

Wait - actually, in Gradio 5.x, the `interactive` parameter defaults to `True` for form components. But the accessibility tree shows `readonly`, which is just how browsers describe radio buttons when they don't have a directly editable value.


The EN radio being checked suggests the browser registered the click, but the server-side change event might not be firing. In Gradio 5.x, `.change()` listeners should work, so I need to figure out why the event isn't propagating.

I'm wondering if the problem is actually with the outputs list in the `lang_radio.change()` call—maybe there's a mismatch between what `on_lang_change` returns and how many outputs are specified, which could cause the event to fail silently. Continuing the count through all the UI components, I've got 38 total outputs being returned from the callback. The `on_lang_change` function then wraps these in a tuple with the language state and the updated interface. Updating UI elements for the knowledge base interface... Finishing the gr.update calls for the remaining UI elements. That accounts for all 38 returns matching the 38 outputs. Now I'm wondering if the issue is with `gr.update(label=...)` on components that have `show_label=False` — that might not actually update anything visible.

Looking at the logs, there's been nothing since the restart, which suggests the `lang_radio.change()` event handler either isn't registering or is firing but silently failing. The event should trigger in Gradio 5.x, so either the handler registration is broken or an exception in `on_lang_change` is being swallowed without logging.

The accessibility snapshot shows the radio button is now checked, meaning the browser registered the click, but Gradio might not be sending the change event to the server. I'm wondering if Gradio 5.x has issues with the massive number of outputs in this event, or if the Tab component labels can't be dynamically updated via `gr.update()` at all. Let me focus on fixing the core issue first—why the change event isn't reaching the backend—and simplify by keeping tab names fixed with language-neutral emoji while only updating the content inside them.

Looking at the page state, the `demo.load` events that should populate the knowledge base status and documents haven't fired either, which suggests the event queue might be blocked by a long initialization process or there's a connectivity issue with Milvus. The labels are showing but no content has been populated, confirming these startup events haven't completed.

Let me test the `on_lang_change` function directly to see if the Radio change event is working properly in Gradio 5.x, since that might reveal whether the event system itself is functioning.

OK so `on_lang_change` returns 38 values. Let me count the outputs list in `lang_radio.change()` to make sure they match:

```python
outputs=[
    lang_state,           # 1
    app_title,            # 2
    app_subtitle,         # 3
    user_input,           # 4
    send_btn,             # 5
    clear_btn,            # 6
    chat_hint,            # 7
    chat_examples_md,     # 8
    *example_btns,        # 9, 10, 11, 12, 13 (5 items)


```

Continuing through the knowledge base section outputs...

```

I'm seeing the rest of the UI component outputs through the ingest log and help markdown sections, totaling 38 return values that match the function's expected outputs. Now I'm checking whether the language radio button's change event is actually firing, since the UI snapshot shows it as readonly.

In Gradio 5, form components are interactive by default, but the accessibility state showing readonly is just standard browser behavior for radio inputs. The real issue might be that no HTTP requests are being sent when I click the radio. Let me check the Docker logs from the beginning to see if there's any activity at all since the container started.

日志显示新代码运行正常（旧日志是之前版本的报错）。语言切换可能已生效但 accessibility 快照未更新。测试知识库 Tab：

I see - the old ui.py errors are still in the logs from before the restart. But the new ui.py is running fine - the last 4 lines show `* Running on local URL: http://0.0.0.0:7860` which means the new code is running.

But the language switch isn't working. Let me look at the docker logs more carefully - the last few logs show the server is running. The issue might be that the event handler for `lang_radio.change` isn't properly registered.

Wait - looking at the accessibility snapshot again. The `lang_radio` uses `readonly` state. In Gradio, when you use `gr.Radio` inside a layout and its `interactive` parameter defaults to True, the browser might still show it as readonly in the accessibility tree. 
 
The click on the radio button might have registered and changed the EN option to checked, but Gradio's WebSocket event queue could be lagging or the event binding isn't firing properly. I might need to reload the page to see if the change actually took effect, or the page was accessed before Gradio finished initializing.

Looking at the logs more carefully, the new ui.py is running and passed syntax checks, but there are no HTTP request logs showing since the restart—which suggests the browser is communicating via WebSocket instead. The real issue might be that the `lang_radio.change` event fired and the DOM updated, but the accessibility tree snapshot captured the state before JavaScript finished updating. Since the EN radio showed as checked, the click did go through. Let me test by switching to the knowledge base tab to see if the content updates accordingly.

知识库 Tab 内容已加载。但 KB 下拉框和语言更新需要确认——先点击"刷新状态"按钮测试后端函数：

The knowledge base tab is now showing correctly with:
- "新知识库名称" textbox
- "＋ 新建知识库" button
- "操作结果" textbox
- "🚀 开始入库" button
- "知识库状态" textbox with placeholder "..."
- "🔄 刷新状态" button
- "🔄 刷新文档列表" button
- "要删除的文件名（逗号分隔）" textbox
- "🗑️ 删除所选文档" button


- The file upload component isn't showing in the accessibility tree, which might be expected for that type of element. I notice the KB status field is empty despite having a placeholder, so the initialization events probably haven't fired yet. More critically, I'm not seeing the knowledge base selector dropdown in the accessible elements—it's likely either failed to load due to no Milvus connection at startup, or the initialization function didn't complete properly.

I'm also noticing the language switch to English didn't update the labels—the text field still shows "新知识库名称" in Chinese even though the language is set to EN. The `on_lang_change` function should have updated this to "New KB name", so let me check the docker logs to see if that event actually fired.

The "刷新状态" button was clicked but the "知识库状态" textbox still shows only the placeholder "...". The response hasn't come back yet. Let me wait and then check again.

Also, I notice that the `kb_selector` dropdown is still not visible in the accessibility tree. This is likely because the component is using `gr.Dropdown` but its accessibility representation might be captured differently.

Let me wait 3 seconds for the server response and take another snapshot.

The logs don't show any new requests being processed. This is strange. The `kb_status_out` is still showing the placeholder.

Wait - I see the issue. Looking at the knowledge base tab layout:

```python
with gr.Row():
    # Left column
    with gr.Column(scale=1):
        kb_selector = gr.Dropdown(...)
        ...
        kb_refresh_btn = gr.Button(T["kb_refresh_btn"], variant="secondary")
```

And then:
```python
kb_refresh_btn.click(
    fn=lambda kb: get_kb_status(kb),
    inputs=kb_state,
    outputs=kb_status_out,
)
```

The `kb_refresh_btn.click` uses `kb_state` as input, but `kb_state` is initialized with `DEFAULT_KB = "insurance_docs"` and only gets updated when `kb_selector.change` triggers `on_kb_select`. The problem is there's no `demo.load` callback to sync the initial state with whatever KB is actually selected, so the refresh button might be querying the wrong knowledge base or stale state.

Looking at the logs, the server has restarted 4 times (I see "Running on local URL" repeated), which suggests there might be crashes happening. The `kb_status_out` still shows placeholder text instead of actual status, which means either the button click isn't firing at all or the server isn't responding to the request. Let me check the recent logs to see if there's a crash pattern.

后端函数工作正常（70 个向量）。查看 Gradio 完整启动日志：

The backend function works! The issue is that the UI button click events aren't getting processed. Let me check if there's a Gradio event queue issue.

Actually, I just realized - the `demo.load()` events might be blocking the event queue. When the page loads, `demo.load(fn=lambda: get_kb_status(DEFAULT_KB), outputs=kb_status_out)` and `demo.load(fn=lambda: list_kb_documents(DEFAULT_KB), outputs=kb_docs_table)` are called. These call Milvus, which might take a while.

In Gradio 5.x, `demo.load` events are processed through the same event queue as user interactions. 

Wait, Gradio actually supports concurrent processing - multiple events can run at the same time unless I've explicitly set `concurrency_limit=1`. So that's probably not the bottleneck. Looking at the accessibility snapshot more carefully, the button shows as active and focused after clicking, but the placeholder text hasn't updated yet. The issue is that the snapshot was taken immediately after the click, before the server had time to respond. I need to wait longer and check again.

Even with a 3-second wait, a simple Milvus query should have completed by now. Let me check the Docker logs to see if there are any HTTP or WebSocket requests actually being processed.

The container is running. But the UI doesn't seem to be responding to button clicks. Let me check if the Docker volume mount (`./ui.py:/app/ui.py:ro`) is being used. In `docker-compose.yml`, there's a volume mount that maps the local `ui.py` to `/app/ui.py`. This is a bind mount, so the container should be using the latest version of the file.

But wait - when we `docker-compose restart ui`, the container restarts but uses the same image. The bind mount means the container sees the current local `ui.py` file. So the latest `ui.py` is being used.


The bind mount in the compose file maps the local `ui.py` directly into the container, so changes are reflected immediately without rebuilding. The import test passed, which confirms the new code is there, but looking at the logs, the Flask server seems to be starting up.

Actually, I'm realizing the "Running on local URL" message appears four times in the logs—likely from four separate container restarts triggered by the `restart: unless-stopped` policy. The first was the original startup, then three more restarts as I was fixing the code issues. The current running instance should be the fourth one with the working code.

The button click still isn't updating the UI though, so let me try refreshing the page to establish a fresh connection and see if that helps.

后端函数本身需 ~11 秒（Milvus 首次连接）。先检查 `rag_ingest.py` 是否有 `ui.py` 需要的所有函数：

OK so after a page refresh, the UI is back to default 简中 language and the chat tab is active. The UI seems to be working fine for navigation. 

The issue with button clicks not updating state might be because:
1. The `demo.load` events are running when the page loads and potentially blocking the queue
2. There's an issue with `kb_state` - when `kb_refresh_btn.click` fires with `inputs=kb_state`, if `kb_state` hasn't been updated from the initial value `DEFAULT_KB`, it should still have `"insurance_docs"` as the value

Actually, wait - I think I understand the issue now. 

The accessibility snapshot I captured was taken before the server response came back. The `get_kb_status('insurance_docs')` function is taking around 11 seconds because it initializes and connects to the Milvus client each time, which is slow on first connection. That's why waiting 3 seconds wasn't enough to see the updated state. I need to try a different approach to avoid this initialization overhead.

Let me just load the page normally in the browser and observe the actual UI with all the updates applied. Or I could navigate to the KB tab, click refresh, wait longer—maybe 15 seconds—then take a snapshot to give the function enough time to complete.

Actually, looking at the code more carefully, I'm wondering if there's an issue with how `kb_state` is being passed to the refresh button callback. The state is initialized with `DEFAULT_KB`, and the button's click handler should pass that state value to `get_kb_status`. But I need to check how `kb_selector.change` is wired up to see if it's properly updating the state when the selection changes.

The real problem is that the page load event is triggering multiple slow Milvus calls simultaneously—one to get the KB status and another to list documents—and the accessibility snapshot is being taken before these async operations finish. So the snapshot captures the initial state rather than the final rendered UI.

Now I'm wondering if the issue might be with how the `rag_ingest` module is exposing its functions—I should check whether `get_milvus_client`, `get_embedder`, `ensure_collection`, `load_pdf_documents`, and `embed_and_insert` are actually being exported properly.
